title: ischnocarpus novae-zelandiae
intro: Ischnocarpus novae-zelandiae, also known as New Zealand holly, is a plant species native to New Zealand. Its berries, leaves, and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Ischnocarpus novae-zelandiae are believed to provide health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. Herbal preparations often involve infusions of the leaves or berries, which can be used to make teas or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
